evidence: The paper uses SHAP values for neuroimaging features and Elastic Net coefficients for polygenic scores—methods not subject to impurity-based bias. While feature rankings are presented (e.g., ENBack task-fMRI contrasts and rs-fMRI as top neuroimaging contributors), the main conclusions concern overall predictive performance (r=0.36-0.54) and variance decomposition percentages from commonality analysis (neuroimaging 66%, PGS 21%), not the magnitude or relative importance of individual features.
